description_long: This is a tall, modern apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a series of repeating balconies running up its facade. The exterior is dominated by alternating bands of dark-tinted glass and concrete, giving it a sleek, contemporary appearance. The building features a prominent central vertical section with large windows, flanked by two slightly protruding wings that house the balconies. The roof is multi-leveled, with a stepped design and mechanical structures visible at the top. The windows are arranged in a highly regular, grid-like pattern, contributing to the building's sense of order and symmetry. The overall structure suggests a high-rise residential tower commonly found in urban cityscapes.
description: A tall, modern apartment tower with repeating balconies, dark glass, and concrete bands, featuring a stepped roof and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: This view highlights the central vertical window section and the left-side balconies, with the stepped roof structure visible at the top.
description_view_1: This angle shows the right-side balconies more prominently and reveals the depth of the building's wings, as well as the central window grid.
description_view_2: The facade's dark glass and concrete bands are most visible here, with the balconies appearing as repeating horizontal elements along the sides.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the symmetry of the window and balcony arrangement, with the stepped roof and mechanical structures clearly visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern, with each floor featuring evenly spaced windows aligned vertically and horizontally across the facade.
